zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Hughes於1507年出生在英國肯特霍斯蒙登時，她的父親Sir John Hughes43歲，母親Lady Frances Hughes43歲。她於1525年9月10日與John James Baldock在英國肯特哈德洛福結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1577年在英國英格蘭肯特霍斯蒙登享年70歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Hughes于1507年出生在英国肯特霍斯蒙登时，她的父亲Sir John Hughes43岁，母亲Lady Frances Hughes43岁。她于1525年9月10日与John James Baldock在英国肯特哈德洛福结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1577年在英国英格兰肯特霍斯蒙登享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hughes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: variant of Hugh with genitival or excrescent -s.
Irish and Scottish: adopted as an equivalent of Gaelic surnames based on the personal name Aodh ‘fire’, for example Ó hAodha, Mac Aodha; see McCoy and compare McHugh .

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Hughes was born in 1507, in Horsmonden, Kent, England, her father, Sir John Hughes, was 43 and her mother, Lady Frances Hughes, was 43. She married John James Baldock on 10 September 1525, in Hadlow, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1577, in Horsmonden, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Hughes est née en 1507, à Horsmonden, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Hughes, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Lady Frances Hughes, avait 43 ans. Elle a épousé John James Baldock le 10 septembre 1525, à Hadlow, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1577, à Horsmonden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Hughes 1507 in Horsmonden, Kent, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Hughes 43 und ihre Mutter Lady Frances Hughes 43. Sie hat am 10. September 1525 in Hadlow, Kent, England John James Baldock geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1577 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Horsmonden, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Hughes è nata il nel 1507 nel Horsmonden, Kent, England, sua padre, Sir John Hughes, aveva 43 anni e sua madre, Lady Frances Hughes, aveva 43 anni. Lei sposato John James Baldock il 10 settembre 1525, nel Hadlow, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1577, nel Horsmonden, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Hughesが1507年にイギリスケントホールスモンデンで生まれた時，父Sir John Hughesは43歳，母Lady Frances Hughesは43歳であった。1525年9月10日にイギリスケントハドロウでJohn James Baldockと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1577年にイギリスイギリスケントホールスモンデンで70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Hughes(이)가 1507년 영국 켄트 호르스몽에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Hughes(은)는 43세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Frances Hughes(은)는 43세였다. 그녀는 1525년 9월 10일 영국 켄트 하들로에서 John James Baldock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1577년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 호르스몽에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Hughes nasceu em 1507, em Horsmonden, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Hughes, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Lady Frances Hughes, tinha 43 anos. Ela casou-se com John James Baldock em 10 de setembro de 1525, em Hadlow, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1577, em Horsmonden, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Hughes родилась в 1507 г. в местности хаурсмонден, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Hughes было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Frances Hughes, 43. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John James Baldock 10 сентября 1525 г. в местности хадлоу, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1577 г. в местности хаурсмонден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Hughes nació en 1507, en Horsmonden, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Hughes, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Lady Frances Hughes, tenía 43 años. Se casó con John James Baldock el 10 de septiembre de 1525, en Hadlow, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1577, en Horsmonden, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 70 años.
